hypothesis: Applying NN-BAX to systems governed by density functional theory rather than Lennard-Jones potentials will reveal that the optimal BAX sampling strategy should be non-uniform along the NEB path—specifically, concentrating query points near predicted transition states rather than sampling uniformly—because the energy/force landscape curvature is highest in these regions, and a curvature-weighted acquisition function will reduce the number of DFT evaluations required for convergence by an additional order of magnitude compared to the uniform random sampling demonstrated in this work.
